Diff two versions of the same branch (spec 027 US7 FR-049/050).

Returns a VersionDiff with branch-level + block-level diffs and a
per-block line_diff for modified blocks. Returns 404 if either version
is missing or the branch doesn't belong to the user. Returns 422 if
v1 == v2 (no-op diff is not useful).

and surfaces the AC-02b tooltip.

  sessions, or doubao sessions where Planner failed
- 500 ``CARD_RENDER_FAILED`` when the renderer raises (AC-22 Edge-7)
- ``X-Card-Cache-Hit: true`` header when served from Redis (AC-24)

GET /api/v1/v2/resumes — list the current user's v2 resumes.

post.description: POST /api/v1/v2/resumes — create a new v2 resume.

get.description:
GET /api/v1/v2/resumes/{id} — fetch a single resume.

T125 — v1 legacy detection. If the row's `data` JSONB has
`data_format_version == 'v1'`, return 400 LEGACY_FORMAT so the
frontend can show a read-only banner + redirect suggestion. New
v2 resumes do not have this key (defaults are v2-shaped).

put.description: PUT /api/v1/v2/resumes/{id}/lock — toggle lock. Independent of version.

post.description:
POST /api/v1/v2/resumes/{id}/duplicate — T158.

Deep-copies the resume's ``data`` JSONB, generates a new UUIDv7,
assigns slug ``<orig>-copy-N`` (N = 1 + max existing N), suffixes
the name with ``" (Copy)"`` (or ``"（副本）"`` for ``zh-CN`` Accept-Language),
and resets ``is_public=false``, ``is_locked=false``,
``password_hash=null``. No statistics or analysis row is copied.

Response shape: ``{"resume": {…full resume data…}}`` so it matches
the create endpoint envelope (locked E2E contract).

put.description:
PUT /api/v1/v2/resumes/{id}/sharing — enable/disable public + password (T141).

Body: ``{"is_public": bool, "password": str | null}``.
  - When ``password`` is a non-empty string, the service bcrypt-hashes
    it at cost 12 and stores the hash. Validation: 6..64 chars.
  - When ``password`` is ``null``, the stored hash is cleared (which
    also requires ``is_public=true`` per the table CHECK constraint).

Returns: ``{"is_public", "password_set", "public_url"}``. The
``public_url`` is ``/r/{display_name}/{slug}`` when public, else ``null``.

get.description:
GET /api/v1/v2/resumes/{id}/statistics — counters + timestamps (T145).

Per spec §2.4: empty ``{views:0, downloads:0, ...}`` when is_public=false
(the row does not exist). Returns 404 NOT_FOUND when the resume
does not exist OR is not owned by the caller.

post.description:
POST /api/v1/v2/resumes/{id}/analyze — T152.

Calls DeepSeek V4 Pro to analyze the resume. The LLM client is
invoked with our own 3-attempt retry loop (1s/2s/4s exponential
backoff). On the 3rd failure the row is stored with
``status='failed'`` + ``failure_reason``; on success it is
UPSERTed with the analysis JSONB.

Per FR-091a there is NO in-memory cache. Each call hits DeepSeek
fresh; the UPSERT is a storage-layer (not a cache) semantic so the
latest row wins.

On both success and failure the ``analysis.completed`` SSE event
is emitted (T155) so connected editors can refresh their Analysis
panel.

get.description:
GET /api/v1/v2/resumes/{id}/analysis — T153.

Returns the latest analysis row (UPSERT — one row per resume) or
404 if the resume has never been analyzed. Ownership pre-flight
returns 404 vs 403 per the v2 contract.

GET /api/v1/v2/public/{username}/{slug} — read-only public resume.

No auth required. If the resume is password-protected and the
caller does not present a valid ``v2_public_pw_<hash>`` cookie,
returns 401 ``PASSWORD_REQUIRED``. On success, increments the
``views`` counter unless the caller is the owner.

post.description:
POST /api/v1/v2/public/{username}/{slug}/verify-password — T143.

Body: ``{"password": str}``. On success: 200 ``{"ok": true}`` +
HttpOnly ``v2_public_pw_<hash>`` cookie (SameSite=Lax, Path=/,
Max-Age=600 seconds = 10 minutes). 401 on bad password.

get.description:
GET /api/v1/v2/public/{username}/{slug}/pdf — public PDF download (T144).

Same cookie flow as ``public_view``. On success: increments
``downloads`` (if caller is not the owner) and returns a JSON
envelope instructing the client to POST the rendered HTML to
``/api/v1/export/render`` (per Wave 12 / US15 client contract).

We deliberately do NOT render server-side here — the export
gateway is the single source of truth for PDF rendering.
Instead, we return the HTML so the client can either:
  (a) POST to /api/v1/export/render (server-side render), or
  (b) use the browser to print-to-PDF client-side.

For a minimal first cut we just return the HTML so callers can
proceed. The full PDF path will be wired in Wave 16 (US14).

post.description:
POST /api/v1/v2/export/render — REQ-036 (US10 T106).

Body: ``{"html": "...", "format": "pdf"|"png"|"jpeg"|"json",
"resume_id": "<uuid>?"}``.

Behavior per contracts/01-rest-api.md §6:
  - ``format: "json"`` → return the resume's full ``ResumeDataV2``
    document as ``application/json`` (ignores ``html``).
  - ``format: "pdf" | "png" | "jpeg"`` → delegate to the existing
    027 export gateway renderer (``render_resume``) so preview↔PDF
    parity is preserved (single rendering path).
  - When ``resume_id`` is supplied, the caller must own the resume;
    on success the ``downloads`` counter is incremented and the
    statistics row is ensured (same shape as the public PDF flow).

Errors:
  - 400 ``EMPTY_CONTENT`` / ``INVALID_FORMAT`` /
    ``CONTENT_TOO_LARGE`` — pre-render validation.
  - 400 ``MISSING_RESUME_ID`` — when ``format=json`` and no
    ``resume_id`` is supplied (no data to return).
  - 403 ``NOT_OWNER`` — caller is not the owner of ``resume_id``.
  - 404 ``NOT_FOUND`` — ``resume_id`` does not exist.
  - 500 ``RENDERING_FAILED`` — Playwright/gateway raised.

get.description:
Return the resume diagnosis panel (US2 FR).

Surfaces the 5 core metrics:
- success rate (count + rate)
- report views (count)
- suggestions shown (count)
- suggestions accepted (count)
- acceptance rate + score delta (avg after - avg before)

Same filter set as overview/funnel. Empty window returns 0 values
+ ``quality_flags.partial_data=true`` + ``freshness_at="unknown"``
per SC-009.

Error mapping (mirrors US1):
- ``ValueError`` -> 400 (bad filter)
- missing/invalid required -> 422 (FastAPI Query validation)

get.description:
Return the mock interview panel (US3 FR).

Surfaces the 5 core metrics:
- session starts (count)
- completions (count)
- completion rate (completions / starts) clamped to [0, 1]
- avg question count (average over completed sessions)
- report views (count)
- retries (count)
- failure rate (failures / starts) clamped to [0, 1]
- failure categories (breakdown by metadata.failure_category)

Same filter set as overview/funnel/resume-diagnosis. Empty window
returns 0 values + ``quality_flags.partial_data=true`` +
``freshness_at="unknown"`` per SC-009.

Error mapping (mirrors US1):
- ``ValueError`` -> 400 (bad filter)
- missing/invalid required -> 422 (FastAPI Query validation)

parameters.name: date_range_start

schema.description: Inclusive ISO 8601 lower bound for the period.

schema.title: Response Get Mock Interview Api V1 Pm Dashboard Metrics Mock Interview Get

get.description:
Return the AI operations panel (US4 FR).

Surfaces the 7 core metrics + 4 top-N breakdowns:
- call_count / success_count / failure_count
- success_rate / failure_rate (clamped to [0, 1])
- retry_count (rows where retry_count > 0)
- p50 / p95 / p99 latency_ms (via Postgres percentile_cont)
- estimated_cost (sum, labeled as estimate per FR-008)
- total_tokens / prompt_tokens / completion_tokens
- model / graph / node / prompt_fingerprint top-5 breakdowns

Same filter set as overview/funnel/resume-diagnosis/mock-interview.
Empty window returns 0 values + ``quality_flags.partial_data=true`` +
``freshness_at="unknown"`` per SC-009.

Privacy: only counts + rates + percentiles + top-N breakdowns are
surfaced. Raw prompt / completion text is never read from
``AIInvocationRecord``.

Error mapping (mirrors US1/US2/US3):
- ``ValueError`` -> 400 (bad filter)
- missing/invalid required -> 422 (FastAPI Query validation)

parameters.name: date_range_start

schema.description: Inclusive ISO 8601 lower bound for the period.

schema.title: Date Range End

parameters.description: Exclusive ISO 8601 upper bound for the period.

schema.title: Response Get Ai Operations Api V1 Pm Dashboard Metrics Ai Operations Get

get.description:
Return the version / experiment panel (US7 FR).

Surfaces 5 metric cards + 2 breakdown tables:

- ``event_count`` — total events contributing to the breakdown.
- 4 distinct counts: ``prompt_fingerprints`` / ``models`` /
  ``app_versions`` / ``experiments``.
- ``top_versions`` table — top 5 rows ordered by count desc, each
  carrying ``prompt_fingerprint × rubric_version × app_version ×
  model``.
- ``top_experiments`` table — top 5 rows ordered by count desc,
  each carrying ``experiment_id``.
- ``trace_available`` flag — when ``False``, the frontend renders
  the "trace unavailable" badge (US7 T123 contract — explicit,
  not silent).

Same filter set as the other 4 panels. Empty window returns 0
values + ``quality_flags.partial_data=true`` + ``freshness_at=
"unknown"`` per SC-009.

Privacy: only counts + breakdowns are surfaced. Raw event content
is never read from the source table.

Error mapping (mirrors US1-US4):
- ``ValueError`` -> 400 (bad filter)
- missing/invalid required -> 422 (FastAPI Query validation)
- internal -> 500

get.description: Get a saved view (FR-006 AC-6.3 + EC-1 + EC-2).

200.description: Saved view detail with role-aware warnings

patch.description: Update a saved view (FR-006 AC-6.4 + EC-3 + SC-009).

200.description: Saved view updated + audit event written

delete.description: Delete a saved view (FR-006 AC-6.5 + SC-009).

204.description: Saved view deleted + audit event written

schema.title: Response Saved Views Health Api V1 Admin Console Saved Views Health Get

AIQualityIssue.title: AIQualityIssue
AIQualityIssue.description:
A single AI quality issue with 8 link fields (FR-018 + AC-18.1/18.2).

Each link is intentionally narrowed so the PM drawer can show all
8 fields in a single read. The schema deliberately avoids raw
prompt / completion payloads (FR-032 privacy) — only identifiers +
verdicts + recommended actions.

AuditEvent.title: AuditEvent
AuditEvent.description: Single audit event row (FR-034 AC-34.2 - 7 fields).

AuditTrailEntry.title: AuditTrailEntry
AuditTrailEntry.description:
A single audit-trail row (EC-4).

The 5 EC-4 fields are all mandatory:

- ``actor`` — who performed the change (user id or role handle).
- ``timestamp`` — ISO 8601 timestamp.
- ``reason`` — human-readable rationale.
- ``before_state`` — snapshot before the change.
- ``after_state`` — snapshot after the change.

AvatarOut.title: AvatarOut
AvatarOut.description: Response shape returned by upload and fetch (mirror) routes.

Badcase.title: Badcase
Badcase.description:
A single badcase row (FR-023).

FR-023 mandated fields (10):

- ``id``
- ``eval_verdict``
- ``affected_feature_area``
- ``affected_user_id``
- ``privacy_class``
- ``classification``
- ``owner``
- ``status`` (open/reviewing/closed/escalated)
- ``resolution``
- ``first_seen_at``

Plus auxiliary fields:

- ``incident_id`` — set when this badcase has been escalated.
- ``freshness_at`` — ISO 8601 OR "unknown".
- ``audit_trail`` — list of audit entries (EC-4 mirror).

BadcaseEscalateResponse.title: BadcaseEscalateResponse
BadcaseEscalateResponse.description: POST /badcases/{id}/escalate response with the new incident_id.

BadcasePromotionRequest.title: BadcasePromotionRequest
BadcasePromotionRequest.description: Request to promote a badcase into an eval dataset lifecycle.

BadcasePromotionResponse.title: BadcasePromotionResponse
BadcasePromotionResponse.description: Eval-case payload produced from a badcase promotion.

BlockDiff.title: BlockDiff
BlockDiff.description:
Per-block diff entry.

`op` is one of 'unchanged' / 'added' / 'removed' / 'modified'.
For `modified` blocks, `line_diff` is populated; for other ops it is null.

BlockLineDiff.title: BlockLineDiff
BlockLineDiff.description:
A single line-level diff entry inside a modified block.

`kind` is one of 'unchanged' / 'added' / 'removed'. Caller renders
them with green/red/grey coloring (FR-050).

stale.title: Stale
stale.description: True if the cohort definition has changed since last computation (EC-2).

CohortSegment.title: CohortSegment
CohortSegment.description:
Reusable cohort / segment definition (FR-013).

``definition`` is a structured (or human-readable) definition; for
Phase 1 we ship the curated string, for Phase 2 batch 2 we will
ship the SQL/JSON definition.
``population`` is the last-computed population size.
``last_computed_at`` is the ISO 8601 timestamp OR ``"unknown"``
per FR-028.

CommandCenterOverview.title: CommandCenterOverview
CommandCenterOverview.description:
Top-level 4 KPI tiles for the workspace header.

Static values for US1 — Phase 2 batch 2 will wire these to the
pm_dashboard overview panel.

CommandCenterOverviewResponse.title: CommandCenterOverviewResponse
CommandCenterOverviewResponse.description: Envelope for the overview KPI tiles.

Comment.title: Comment
Comment.description: A single comment on an incident.

CommentCreateRequest.title: CommentCreateRequest
CommentCreateRequest.description: POST body for adding a comment to an incident.

accepted_patch_indices.title: Accepted Patch Indices
accepted_patch_indices.description: Indices of patches to apply (0-based). None = apply all. Only valid with decision=apply.

CostFeatureBreakdown.title: CostFeatureBreakdown
CostFeatureBreakdown.description: One row in the per-area cost breakdown (FR-016).

CostQualityFlag.title: CostQualityFlag
CostQualityFlag.description:
Cost-vs-quality tradeoff signal (FR-019 + AC-19.1/19.2/19.3).

``cost_delta_pct`` is the percentage change vs the previous period.
``quality_delta_pct`` is the percentage change in the weighted
success-rate vs the previous period. When
``cost_delta_pct > threshold`` AND ``quality_delta_pct < 0`` (cost
up + quality down), the seed surfaces
``severity == "critical"`` so the UI renders the red banner.

stale.title: Stale
stale.description: True if last_reconciled_at is older than the 14d stale threshold (EC-3).

CostSummaryResponse.title: CostSummaryResponse
CostSummaryResponse.description:
Envelope for GET /ai-operations/cost-summary.

``last_reconciled_at`` is the ISO 8601 timestamp of the last
cost-rate table reconciliation; the seed uses ``_earlier_iso(7)``
so EC-3 can be observed. When the seed date is older than the
``COST_RECONCILIATION_STALE_DAYS`` threshold, the frontend renders
the "cost estimate outdated" flag.

CurrentValue.title: CurrentValue
CurrentValue.description: Single live current metric value (FR-030 AC-30.1).

DecisionSignal.title: DecisionSignal
DecisionSignal.description:
A single prioritized item in the command-center decision queue.

FR-008 mandated fields (10):

- ``id`` — stable signal identifier.
- ``category`` — one of the 6 FR-007 categories.
- ``what_changed`` — plain-language description of the change.
- ``affected_segment`` — segment / cohort the change applies to.
- ``comparison_baseline`` — comparison period label + summary.
- ``severity`` — critical / high / medium / low / info.
- ``confidence`` — confirmed / sampled / inferred / candidate.
- ``owner`` — current owner or suggested owner.
- ``freshness_at`` — ISO 8601 timestamp OR the literal ``"unknown"``.
- ``next_review_link`` — link to the next review surface.

Plus:

- ``quality_flags`` — :class:`SignalQualityFlags` for FR-028.
- ``evidence_links`` — :class:`list[EvidenceLink]` for FR-018.
- ``priority`` — numeric sort key (higher = more urgent).
- ``detected_at`` — when the signal was first raised.
- ``headline_metric_id`` — optional pointer to the underlying
  metric_id (e.g. ``"overview.ai_success_rate"``) for downstream
  drilldown (Phase 2 batch 3).

high_severity_count.title: High Severity Count
high_severity_count.description: Count of severity in {critical, high}; drives FR-010 quiet-state.

path.title: Path
path.description: JSON-path-like location, e.g. 'input.messages[2].content'. B1 emits only 1-level dotted paths under the node name; deeper segments land with 039-polish.

DiffFieldEntry.title: DiffFieldEntry
DiffFieldEntry.description:
One field-level change in a node diff.

NOTE: For B1 the diff engine is 1-level deep — ``path`` will only
contain a single ``.``-separated segment under the node prefix
(e.g. ``node1.temperature``). The example in ``description`` shows
the eventual FR-015 shape; deeper nested paths are a TODO for the
039-polish batch.

EvalBadcaseSummary.title: EvalBadcaseSummary
EvalBadcaseSummary.description:
Workspace eval + badcase summary card (FR-020 + AC-20.1/20.2).

``recent_badcases`` MUST have ≥5 entries (AC-20.2). The seed
surfaces 6 so the UI doesn't have to special-case empty.

EvalRunSummary.title: EvalRunSummary
EvalRunSummary.description:
Recent eval run + pass_rate summary (FR-020 + AC-20.1).

Per US3 the eval layer is READ-ONLY: this surfaces high-level
counts (total_eval_runs + pass_rate) so PM does not have to open
developer-only logs.

ExperimentCompareRequest.title: ExperimentCompareRequest
ExperimentCompareRequest.description: Baseline/candidate eval reports for REQ-045 comparison.

ExperimentCompareResponse.title: ExperimentCompareResponse
ExperimentCompareResponse.description: Quality/cost/latency comparison result for AI Ops.

ExportRenderIn.title: ExportRenderIn
ExportRenderIn.description:
POST body for ``/api/v1/v2/export/render``.

The pipeline accepts pre-rendered HTML (same shape as
``/api/v1/export/render``) plus an optional ``resume_id`` so we
can attribute the download + bump the counter. When ``format`` is
``"json"`` the ``html`` field is ignored and the resume's full
``ResumeDataV2`` document is returned verbatim.

NOTE: ``html`` length validation is intentionally NOT a
Pydantic-constraint here. We want the handler to return the
flat ``413 CONTENT_TOO_LARGE`` envelope (per the v2 contract)
rather than a 422 from Pydantic's ``max_length`` check. The
handler does the size check before delegating to the gateway.

ExportRequest.title: ExportRequest
ExportRequest.description:
HTML-based export payload (spec 027 US1).

`markdown` and `style_id` were removed — the front-end renders markdown
to HTML via the unified render engine before posting.

FailureCategoryBreakdown.title: FailureCategoryBreakdown
FailureCategoryBreakdown.description: One row in the failure-category breakdown (FR-016).

FeatureAdoptionMetric.title: FeatureAdoptionMetric
FeatureAdoptionMetric.description:
One metric within the 5-metric grid (FR-014).

Discovery / first use / repeat / frequency / downstream outcome.
Each carries current value + comparison_period delta + sample_size.
``insufficient_data`` is True when sample_size < threshold (EC-3).

FrozenValue.title: FrozenValue
FrozenValue.description: Single frozen metric value (FR-029 SC-012 AC-29.1).

FunnelComparisonDelta.title: FunnelComparisonDelta
FunnelComparisonDelta.description:
Comparison-period delta (FR-012).

``comparison_period_label`` is the human label (e.g. "前 7 天").
``step_conversion_delta`` is the per-step conversion delta vs the
comparison period (0..1, signed). Aggregated at the response
envelope level for the headline metric.

FunnelResponse.title: FunnelResponse
FunnelResponse.description:
Funnel payload (FR-012).

``entry_conversion`` is the share of total users who entered the
first step (0..1).
``steps`` is the ordered list of 5 funnel rows (AC-12.1).
``time_to_convert`` is the optional P50 + CI band (AC-12.3).
``comparison_delta`` is the optional comparison-period delta
(AC-12.2).
``cohort_id`` + ``cohort_population`` + ``last_computed_at`` are
surfaced per AC-13.3.

FunnelStep.title: FunnelStep
FunnelStep.description:
One funnel step row (FR-012).

``count`` is the number of users who reached this step.
``step_conversion`` is the conversion from the previous step
(0..1, optional if first step).
``drop_off`` is the drop-off from the previous step (0..1,
optional if first step).

candidate.title: Candidate
candidate.description: EC-1: True if this is a low-confidence anomaly not yet merged into confirmed incidents.

common_root_cause.title: Common Root Cause
common_root_cause.description: EC-2: human-readable root-cause label when this incident shares a root cause with siblings.

linked_incident_ids.title: Linked Incident Ids
linked_incident_ids.description: EC-2: cross-link to sibling incidents sharing common_root_cause.

ingestion_delayed.title: Ingestion Delayed
ingestion_delayed.description: EC-3: True when data ingestion lag caused the incident.

Incident.title: Incident
Incident.description:
A single incident row (FR-021 + Edge Cases EC-1/2/3).

FR-021 mandated fields (10):
- ``id``
- ``title``
- ``severity`` (P0/P1/P2/P3)
- ``status`` (open/investigating/resolved/postmortem)
- ``owner``
- ``affected_feature_area``
- ``affected_journey_step``
- ``first_seen_at``
- ``last_seen_at``
- ``trend`` (rising/stable/declining)

Edge case fields:

- ``candidate`` (EC-1) — True iff this is a low-confidence anomaly
  that has NOT been merged into the confirmed incident list. The
  frontend MUST display a "candidate" label and not surface it
  alongside confirmed incidents.
- ``common_root_cause`` (EC-2) — non-empty when this incident
  shares a technical root cause with one or more other incidents
  (different journey steps but same root cause).
- ``linked_incident_ids`` (EC-2) — cross-link to sibling incidents
  sharing ``common_root_cause``.
- ``ingestion_delayed`` (EC-3) — True when data ingestion lag
  caused the incident (vs. real product behavior). Frontend MUST
  surface an "ingestion delayed" label.
- ``freshness_at`` — ISO 8601 timestamp OR literal "unknown".
- ``affected_count`` — number of users / tasks / events affected.
- ``audit_trail`` — list of audit entries (EC-4).

confirmed_count.title: Confirmed Count
confirmed_count.description: Count of candidate=False incidents; drives the FR-021 + EC-1 separation.

candidate_count.title: Candidate Count
candidate_count.description: Count of candidate=True incidents; the EC-1 separation count.

KPIBundle.title: KPIBundle
KPIBundle.description:
4 workspace-header KPI tiles (FR-016 + AC-16.1).

All values are positive; ``success_rate`` and ``quality_score`` are
clamped to [0, 1]. ``freshness_at`` is ISO 8601 or the literal
``"unknown"`` sentinel per FR-028 (Edge Case line 317).

MetricDefinition10Field.title: MetricDefinition10Field
MetricDefinition10Field.description:
Full 10-field metric trust definition (FR-027).

The 10 fields are: definition / owner / source / numerator /
denominator / unit / period / freshness / completeness /
quality_flags. All string fields default to NOT_PROVIDED so the
frontend can render the literal ``"(not provided)"`` instead of
silently dropping a column (AC-27.4).

PromptProposalCreateRequest.title: PromptProposalCreateRequest
PromptProposalCreateRequest.description: Create a REQ-045 prompt improvement proposal.

PromptProposalResponse.title: PromptProposalResponse
PromptProposalResponse.description: Prompt proposal response for PM/admin review.

QuestionTemplate.title: QuestionTemplate
QuestionTemplate.description:
A single question-first template card (FR-011).

A template is a curated question PM can ask with one click. The
actual aggregation logic is owned by ``service.list_question_templates``
which returns ≥3 templates per tab (AC-11.2).

entity_updated_at.title: Entity Updated At
entity_updated_at.description: Client's last-known updated_at for conflict detection

client_timestamp.title: Client Timestamp
client_timestamp.description: Unix ms timestamp of client operation

ResumeV2UpdateIn.title: ResumeV2UpdateIn
ResumeV2UpdateIn.description:
PUT body — partial document update.

REQ-039: ``data`` is a free-form ``dict`` (not the strict
``ResumeDataV2Pydantic``) so a frontend PUT that only touches
one sub-tree (e.g. just ``metadata.template``) doesn't 422 on
the required sub-fields. The service layer (``merge_resume_data``)
stitches the partial into the stored full doc, validates the
template id with a graceful fallback, and only then persists.

reason.title: Reason
reason.description: Justification for the reveal - must be 20+ chars (FR-033 AC-33.4)

comparison_period.title: Comparison Period
comparison_period.description: Human-readable comparison baseline label, e.g. 'vs prior week'

annotations.title: Annotations
annotations.description: PM commentary that gets baked into the snapshot (FR-029 SC-012)

ReviewSnapshotResponse.title: ReviewSnapshotResponse
ReviewSnapshotResponse.description:
POST + GET /review-snapshots/{id} response (FR-029/030 + SC-012).

The 8 SC-012 fields (frozen_values / comparison_deltas /
metric_definitions / freshness_warnings / quality_flags /
evidence_links / filters / annotations) are all required
non-empty by AC-SC-12.1.

SavedView.title: SavedView
SavedView.description:
Single saved-view row (FR-006 AC-6.1).

The 12 fields match the GET /saved-views response shape:

- ``id`` (str) — unique id (``sv-{seq:06d}``).
- ``name`` (str) — human-readable label.
- ``workspace_id`` (WorkspaceId) — primary workspace.
- ``filters`` (dict) — opaque JSON filter state.
- ``owner_user_id`` (str) — UUID-as-string of the owner.
- ``description`` (str) — free-text description.
- ``trust_status`` (SavedViewTrustStatus) — 3-state trust.
- ``created_at`` (str) — ISO timestamp.
- ``updated_at`` (str) — ISO timestamp.
- ``shared_with`` (list[SharedWithRole]) — roles with access
  (empty list + owner-only-implicit → private to owner).
- ``version`` (int) — optimistic-locking counter (EC-3).
- ``warnings`` (list[str]) — render-time warnings (deleted
  cohort, permission revoked, etc.).

SavedViewCreateResponse.title: SavedViewCreateResponse
SavedViewCreateResponse.description:
POST /saved-views response (FR-006 AC-6.2).

Includes the new view + the audit event id so the frontend can
show ``audit_event_id`` in the row's tooltip.

SavedViewDetailResponse.title: SavedViewDetailResponse
SavedViewDetailResponse.description:
GET /saved-views/{id} detail envelope (FR-006 AC-6.3).

Extends SavedView with role-aware warnings (EC-1 deleted-cohort,
EC-2 permission-revoked) so the frontend can render the
``filter references deleted cohort, please update`` or
``permission revoked`` banner.

SavedViewUpdateRequest.title: SavedViewUpdateRequest
SavedViewUpdateRequest.description:
PATCH /saved-views/{id} body (FR-006 AC-6.4).

All fields optional so a partial update can be sent. ``version``
is required for optimistic locking (EC-3): if the current row
version does not match, the API returns 422 ``version_conflict``.

stale.title: Stale
stale.description: True if freshness_at exceeds the metric definition target.

partial_baseline.title: Partial Baseline
partial_baseline.description: True if the comparison period is incomplete (Edge Case line 322).

delayed_ingestion.title: Delayed Ingestion
delayed_ingestion.description: True if the underlying data ingestion lags behind real time.

no_data.title: No Data
no_data.description: True if the signal exists but the underlying metric has zero rows.

SignalQualityFlags.title: SignalQualityFlags
SignalQualityFlags.description:
Quality flags for a single decision signal (FR-009 / FR-028).

Mirrors pm_dashboard.QualityFlags semantics adapted for the
decision-queue scope. All flags default to False / empty so the
backend can return a signal even when telemetry is missing.

tag.title: Tag
tag.description: Tag text; 1-50 chars; charset = letters / digits / _ / - / CJK / space.

TaskTagCreateRequest.title: TaskTagCreateRequest
TaskTagCreateRequest.description:
POST body for ``POST .../tasks/{id}/tags``.

Tag must be 1-50 chars and match the regex in E5.

TaskTagListResponse.title: TaskTagListResponse
TaskTagListResponse.description: GET /tasks/{id}/tags response — list of the caller's tags.

TaskTagOut.title: TaskTagOut
TaskTagOut.description: GET / POST / DELETE response shape for a single tag.

TimeToConvertBand.title: TimeToConvertBand
TimeToConvertBand.description:
Time-to-convert summary band (FR-012).

``p50_seconds`` is the median time-to-convert (seconds).
``ci95_lower_seconds`` + ``ci95_upper_seconds`` define the 95%
confidence interval.
``sample_size`` is the number of converted users in the cohort;
a sample_size below the FR-028 / EC-3 threshold should be
rendered with an "Insufficient data" badge.

TokenUsageRow.title: TokenUsageRow
TokenUsageRow.description: One row in the token-usage stacked bar (FR-016).

UserPrivacySafe.title: UserPrivacySafe
UserPrivacySafe.description:
Privacy-safe user profile (FR-015 + FR-032).

The schema deliberately ONLY carries the 7 allow-listed field names.
raw_resume / raw_interview_answer / raw_prompt / raw_model_output
are NOT exposed; the Frontend AC-15.4 grep must return 0 hits when
applied to ``UsersAccounts.tsx``.

UserPrivacySafeField.title: UserPrivacySafeField
UserPrivacySafeField.description: A single privacy-safe field row with visibility level (AC-15.3).

VersionDiff.title: VersionDiff
VersionDiff.description: Top-level diff payload returned by GET /versions/:v1/diff/:v2.

VersionDimensionAvailability.title: VersionDimensionAvailability
VersionDimensionAvailability.description:
Per-dimension availability (FR-017 + EC-2).

Each dimension carries a list of known values + an ``unknown_count``
so the UI can render "version unknown" explicitly for legacy rows
(Edge Case line 322). NOT silent-fallback to the baseline.

VolumeByFeatureRow.title: VolumeByFeatureRow
VolumeByFeatureRow.description: One row in the volume-by-feature chart (FR-016).

app__modules__admin_console__decision_si.title: EvidenceLink
app__modules__admin_console__decision_si.description:
A single evidence link attached to a signal.

FR-008 says each signal MUST include ``next_review_link``; we also
surface an ``evidence_links`` array per FR-018 so AI-quality signals
can attach eval/badcase/log references.

Fields:

- ``label`` — human-readable label rendered in the drawer.
- ``href`` — deep-link route (e.g. ``/admin-console/ai-operations?from=signal-001``).
- ``kind`` — semantic class for icon selection
  (review / eval / badcase / log / trace / metric / report).
- ``privacy_class`` — visibility tier; ``public`` is shown to PM,
  ``internal`` to operations, ``restricted`` to maintainer only.

app__modules__admin_console__incidents__.title: EvidenceLink
app__modules__admin_console__incidents__.description:
A single evidence link attached to an incident or badcase (FR-022).

The 8-type ``type`` Literal mirrors the 8 FR-022 evidence surfaces
(product metrics / user impact / AI tasks / eval cases / logs /
traces / releases / comments). ``reference_id`` is the canonical
cross-workspace id (e.g. ``"metric:funnel.registered_to_first_interview"``
or ``"task:019ec1be-..."``).

app__modules__admin_console__review_snap.title: EvidenceLink
app__modules__admin_console__review_snap.description:
Privacy-safe evidence reference (FR-029 + FR-032 SC-010).

``label`` is a human-readable summary; the link is an opaque id
reference (no raw payload ever embedded).
